audit_items.proof_trace: The return value of `freenect_init(&ctx, 0)` at line 220 is completely discarded; no NULL check on `ctx` follows before `freenect_select_subdevices(ctx, ...)` at line 224. `freenect_init` (core.c:40-58) explicitly sets `*ctx = NULL` on both failure paths (malloc failure and `fnusb_init` failure), and `freenect_select_subdevices` (core.c:133-136) dereferences `ctx` unconditionally via `ctx->enabled_subdevices = ...`, causing a NULL pointer dereference (SIGSEGV).
audit_items.attack_scenario: If `freenect_init` fails due to USB initialization error or memory allocation failure, any call to the public sync API (e.g., `freenect_sync_get_video`, `freenect_sync_get_depth`) triggers `setup_kinect()` → `init_thread()`, which calls `freenect_select_subdevices(NULL, ...)`, crashing the process; the subsequently spawned `init` thread would also crash at `freenect_process_events(NULL)` in core.c:82.
